Ad Text:
A new transformational adventure awaits you… 

How does 5 weeks of annual leave, a fulfilling career you'll genuinely enjoy, and working for a reputable organization that promotes wellbeing and drives digital transformation sound to you?

As one of Forbes 2024 ‘World’s Best Employers’ and Fortune’s ‘World’s Most Admired Companies’ for 2025, Zurich - a leader in the insurance industry, is on a journey towards a brighter future… A brighter future for you - focused on your career, your wellbeing, and your community - as well as our customers and the planet.

Join us on this thrilling journey as we work towards our vision of becoming Australia's leading digital insurer. If you're eager to be part of a values-driven global organisation that's experiencing real growth and transformation, and if you share our commitment to making a tangible difference and taking continuous steps towards a sustainable future for our people, customers, communities, and our planet, then keep reading!

Let’s create a brighter future together, let’s make a difference  

It’s important to know, what you do matters. At Zurich, we don’t just cover, we care.

As a Squad Tech Lead within our Zx initiative, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping the future of our AI‑first engineering capabilities. Sitting at the heart of a modern product‑led environment, this role exists to lead technical strategy, uplift engineering standards, and create scalable, secure solutions that unlock meaningful customer and business outcomes. You’ll guide a talented squad of engineers, partnering closely with product and design to take ideas from exploration through to production, while helping build Zurich’s next generation of agentic AI capabilities.

This is a unique opportunity to work in a greenfield environment with the autonomy to innovate, experiment, and influence how we deliver cutting‑edge AI experiences across the organisation. Your work will have direct impact, supporting our mission to reimagine insurance and build a modern, cloud‑native ecosystem that delivers long‑term value for customers and our people.

You will also be responsible for the following:

Leading end-to-end AI agent development from discovery through to reliable production outcomes
Setting and uplifting AI engineering standards, practices and ways of working across squads
Driving secure, scalable and observable AI-native solutions that perform in production
Partnering closely with product, design and business stakeholders to solve meaningful problems
Supporting and mentoring engineers to build capability, confidence and engineering excellence
Championing reuse, shared platforms and frameworks to accelerate delivery and consistency

Important to your success – let’s grow together

You’re someone who thrives in an environment of curiosity, experimentation, and high accountability. You enjoy solving complex problems with others, bring a thoughtful and collaborative approach to decision making, and are energised by leading teams towards meaningful outcomes.

In order to be successful, you will have the following:

Extensive experience leading full stack engineering in cloud-native environments
Proven hands-on experience building and running AI or agentic systems in production
Strong capability across modern AI engineering, including orchestration, evaluation and observability
Deep experience with DevSecOps, CI/CD and production ownership in regulated environments
Solid cloud engineering experience, ideally across AWS and infrastructure as code
Experience working in product-led, agile teams delivering customer-focused outcomes

Belong. Zurich is here to support you 

There are so many reasons why Zurich is a great place to be and the right choice for you, but here are just a few.

Enjoy 5 weeks of annual leave for permanent staff through our Z leave initiative, along with 15 days of personal/carer’s leave as we empower our people to manage their own wellbeing. Plus, access to Family Care (parental leave).
Benefit from a hybrid working arrangement – 3 days in the office and 2 days working from home per week for full-time employees.
Access programs to stay healthy and feel healthier, receive an AUD$500 wellbeing payment per year, and a AUD$295 new starter e-voucher for your work-from-home setup (flex work - hybrid working), along with other great benefits through our Shine portal.
Grow and thrive in your career with our award-winning training programs. We support and encourage your development because our strength lies in our people, and we value your individuality.
Work with global, diverse, and inclusive teams; explore your passions, fuel innovation, drive equity, collaborate, and make a positive difference beyond your role.
Take advantage of global mobility opportunities across more than 200 countries worldwide.
Volunteer and work in our state-of-the-art Green Star rated offices (when you are in the office) and dress for your day.
We plant a tree for every new employee.
Not to mention our various employer of choice awards/memberships – WGEA; Family Inclusive Workplace; Gold AWEI Employer, Pride in Diversity… to name a few.

We could go on, but the main point is that Zurich is a great place to be, where you can truly belong, be yourself, maintain work/life balance, and thrive in a supportive environment.

Zurich is an equal opportunity employer.  We are committed to ensuring that our recruitment process is fair and accessible for all candidates. If you require any special accommodations to participate in our recruitment process, we encourage you to please let us know at the time of your application.  

So, if you want to come on an adventure that will build a brighter future together, and feel a sense of togetherness, then please apply today to start your adventure!

At the National Transport Research Organisation (NTRO) we strive to be an innovation portal through which government and industry can gain great outcomes across all modes of transport. We create new knowledge through research, develop the means for improving practice, and provide expert advice to the Australia and New Zealand Transport Agencies and industry to ensure that our communities will always have safe, sustainable and efficient transportation networks.

NTRO is a globally connected Not-for-profit, purpose and innovation-driven organisation that has over many decades built a strong presence within the Roads, Rail, Ports and Airports sectors which ensures that we cover all modes of the transport network in everything we do.

Your next opportunity awaits at NTRO
At NTRO, we believe in building technology that moves industries forward. You'll work alongside a multidisciplinary team of engineers, data scientists, and innovators — all dedicated to developing systems that bridge the physical and digital worlds.

Are you passionate about the intersection of hardware, software, and data? Do you thrive on turning intelligent machines into smarter, more connected systems? NTRO's Data and Technology team are seeking a Senior Mechatronics Software Engineer to help design, develop, and optimise the intelligent mechatronic platforms that power our world-class data capture vehicles and related technologies.

In this role, you'll work at the cutting edge of embedded software, control systems and data-driven engineering, shaping the way machines interact with their environment and transform data into insight.

This role is full-time and based in our Port Melbourne office.

Main duties of the role
In this role, you will be a key point of contact, supporting technical direction and delivery outcomes for the team.

Problem Solving: Proven capability in problem solving and critical thinking, including breaking down technical challenges, evaluating options & recommending the best outcome forward
Software & Systems Development: Design, develop, and maintain embedded software and firmware for smart devices, automation systems, and connected mechatronic platforms.
Data Integration: Implement data collection, logging, and communication between hardware components and cloud or edge systems to enable analysis and optimisation.
Control Systems Engineering: Develop and tune advanced control algorithms (e.g. PID, model predictive, adaptive control) for sensors, actuators, and robotic systems.
Connectivity & IoT: Integrate communication protocols such as CAN, Modbus, and TCP/IP to support real-time data exchange in networked systems.
Simulation & Testing: Model and simulate mechatronic systems using tools such as MATLAB/Simulink, LabVIEW, or Python, and perform hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing for validation.
Collaboration & Data Utilisation: Partner with data engineers and scientists to feed operational data into analytics or machine learning pipelines, supporting predictive maintenance and system optimisation.
Documentation & Compliance: Prepare detailed documentation of system architecture, software design, and data flow processes while ensuring alignment with organisational standards, safety, and quality requirements.
About you
You are a highly skilled and curious engineer who thrives in multidisciplinary environments? Drawing on your experience leading teams and your hands-on approach to solving complex technical challenges, you turn data into meaningful insights that elevate system performance and drive innovation forward.

Essential Skills and Experience

Relevant tertiary qualification in Mechatronics, Software, Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or related discipline
5+ years of relevant experience in mechatronic software and systems engineering
Proven experience applying critical thinking and problem solving to a range of technical challenges
Experience with leading, supervising or mentoring junior engineers/ teams previously
Proficiency in Python, C++, SQL, and firmware development.
Experience with ROS (Robot Operating System), PLC programming, and industrial automation frameworks.
Knowledge of machine learning / AI integration in mechatronic systems, including image recognition.
Familiarity with digital twins, predictive analytics, and data visualisation.
Understanding of cybersecurity principles for connected devices.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a focus on system reliability and data quality.
Excellent communication, teamwork, and documentation capabilities.
We offer the following and more to ensure we look after the ones who matter most, Our People

Opportunities to work with globally recognised experts, who offer one-on-one mentorship and support throughout your career.
Excellent employee benefits including access to pre-tax salary sacrifice arrangements to make your take home pay work better for you and your family
Access to 8 weeks of parental leave and support upon your return to the workplace
Annual Christmas closure with an additional 2 paid public holidays in December because we recognise taking time with those close to us is important
Tailored career development that supports your aspirations with a clear step-by-step guide for progression within the organisation
Reward and recognition programs to highlight the great work that is being done every day from our incredible team members
Well established social club in almost all offices with regular activities, quiz nights and tournaments to keep you busy!
Every role at NTRO is meaningful, are you ready for yours?
At NTRO we are proud to continually attract leading minds within the Transport infrastructure and research areas. We host a culturally and gender diverse work environment and we celebrate workforce diversity while highlighting the multidisciplinary teams of professionals from all over the world. We strongly encourage applications from Women looking to make an impact within the STEM arena.

Don't meet every single requirement for this role?
We recognise you may have a diverse set of skills, work experiences, and life experiences that can greatly enhance the ability to deliver effective transport solutions and positive community outcomes. If you are excited about this role, but your experience doesn't align with the criteria we still encourage you to submit your application.

NTRO actively seeks to include and value the unique talents, perspectives, experiences, and contributions of all applicants.

Applicants must be residing Australian Citizens / Permanent Resident of Australia.
Please ensure you attached your CV, attach a separate cover letter (no more than one page) addressing why you would be perfect for this role and what you would be hoping to get out of it, and answer the screening questions provided.

Permanent - Full Time

Sydney (Head Office)

Who we are…

Founded in 2002, ELMO Software is the trusted provider of HR technology solutions to 2,000+ mid-sized organisations and more than one million end users across Australia and New Zealand.With a comprehensive suite of ISO-certified solutions that span the full employee lifecycle, ELMO Software is designed to scale as organisations grow. Flexible and configurable, ELMO’s one-stop HRIS fits to your specific needs and workflows.Through powerful technology, automation, data and analytics, ELMO Software empowers HR professionals to play an integral role in company decision making.

Life & Culture...

At ELMO you will be joining an epic team with the opportunity to make a big impact. We foster an authentic, collaborative, fun working environment and being an ELMOnian means you're part of an amazing team that will push you to grow, support you with challenges and allow you to shine. Some of our benefits include:

Hybrid work set up
Paid Parental leave - 12 weeks primary, 6 weeks secondary
Birthday leave, YOU day each year, as well as connecting people leave (up to 6 weeks working from anywhere)
Inhouse Learning and Development initiatives
ELMO Social and Diversity clubs
Wellbeing initiatives such as boot camp, yoga etc.
Mental Health/EAP programs
Flare Benefits (great discounts, novated leasing, salary sacrifice)

Our values:

Reimagine What's Possible - We believe innovation is human at its core. By staying open, fearless, and adaptive, we continuously push boundaries - while keeping people at the heart of everything we do.
Obsess over Customers – Everything we do is designed to positively impact our customers.
Help Others Thrive - Be they colleagues, communities or customers, we champion ways to help others thrive.
Be Fearlessly Optimistic - We bring unwavering positivity to any challenge, as we know it will drive meaningful change.

Please note: Our internal Talent Acquisition team has got this covered, we’re not open to using an agency for this role.

As our Lead AI Engineer, you'll spearhead the development of cutting-edge agentic AI solutions that automate complex HR processes, enhance employee experiences, and deliver unprecedented business intelligence.

This isn't just another AI engineering role; it's an opportunity to pioneer intelligent automation in HR technology while leading a world-class team.

Responsibilities:

Lead AI Architecture: Design and implement enterprise-scale AI systems
Build Agentic AI Solutions: Create intelligent agents that automate tasks
Drive Innovation: Create innovative solutions through the use of AI
Scale Intelligence: Transform our existing automation platform with advanced AI capabilities

Revolutionary AI Development:

Design and deploy agentic AI workflows that autonomously handle complex HR scenarios
Build intelligent automation agents using Large Language Models, RAG architectures, and multi-agent systems
Create real-time AI processing pipelines that enhance every aspect of the employee experience
Develop AI-powered integrations with 50+ SaaS platforms through our existing automation infrastructure

Technical Leadership:

Lead a growing AI team of ML/AI engineers and automation specialists
Mentor and develop junior engineers while building ELMO's AI centre of excellence
Drive technical decisions that will shape ELMO's AI strategy for years to come

Platform Innovation:

Integrate AI capabilities into our existing automation platform
Build scalable ML infrastructure on existing cloud infrastructure using the latest AI/ML services
Implement responsible AI practices ensuring privacy, fairness, and compliance with global regulations
Design APIs and SDKs that enable customers to build their own AI-enhanced workflows

Core AI/ML Expertise:

7+ years software engineering with 5+ years in AI/ML systems
3+ years leading technical teams (hiring, mentoring, performance management)
Deep hands-on experience with Large Language Models (OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS Bedrock)
Production deployment of 2+ generative AI applications
Strong knowledge of RAG architectures, vector databases, embedding models
Experience with agentic frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen, CrewAI)

Technical Leadership Skills:

Track record architecting complex AI projects from concept to production
Expertise in MLOps: model versioning, A/B testing, monitoring, retraining
Advanced Python with modern ML frameworks (PyTorch/TensorFlow)
Cloud ML platform experience (AWS SageMaker strongly preferred)
Understanding of enterprise SaaS, APIs, microservices, integration patterns
Experience with CI/CD, containerization, infrastructure as code

Leadership Qualities:

Team builder: Experience hiring, mentoring, and developing AI/ML talent
Communicator: Ability to translate complex AI concepts for business stakeholders
Innovator: Track record of identifying and implementing emerging AI technologies
Results-driven: Focus on measurable business outcomes and customer impact

About Ticketek Entertainment Group​

Ticketek Entertainment Group is a global fan experience Company that tickets, promotes and delivers incredible live experiences that are impossible to forget.  In a distracted world where nothing beats real human moments, We make life better live!

Our Group includes; our Fan Experience Platform (Ticketek) that sells tickets and provides value added services, Event promoting with businesses across Touring (TEG Touring), Sport (TEG Sport), and Family Experiences (TEG Experiences) and our digital business (Ovation) which focuses on delivering seamless data-driven outcomes for our fans and partners

About the Role: 

As a ML engineer, We are seeking a highly experienced Senior Engineer to lead the design and evolution of our world-class recommendation and decision engine. You will be responsible for building a "Single Customer View" platform that integrates real-time data pipelines, Vertex AI, and transactional API solutions. A core focus of this role is leading the transition from legacy architectures to modern, serverless cloud environments, utilizing Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) to ensure the platform is scalable, cost-efficient, cyber and GDPR-compliant



Requirements



What does a day in the life look like?

ML Design & Cloud Architecture

Production-Ready ML & MLOps: Partner with Data Scientists to transition experimental models into robust production services; design automated, scalable pipelines for training, evaluation, and deployment using Vertex AI.
Data Warehouse & SCV: Architect highly scalable Cloud Data Warehouses and Single Customer View logic using Python, Go, and SQL to power real-time decisioning and unified customer profiles.
Smart Orchestration: Manage complex workflow automation using Cloud Composer (Airflow) and Cloud Workflows, prioritizing the migration to fully managed services.

Data Engineering & Analytics

High-Performance Pipelines: Design streaming data applications using Dataflow (Apache Beam), Cloud Run, and Pub/Sub, including API integrations with robust retry mechanisms.
Hybrid Database Management: Build optimized data models in BigQuery and AlloyDB to support both high-speed transactional queries and analytical insights via Looker.
Site Reliability (SRE): Apply SRE principles to diagnose faults in distributed systems and implement advanced monitoring via Google Cloud Operations Suite.

DevOps, Security & FinOps

Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Manage multi-cloud (GCP/AWS) infrastructure using Terraform and GitHub Actions, ensuring all provisioning follows strict version-control and CI/CD principles.
Security & Compliance: Implement secure authentication frameworks (e.g., Workload Identity Federation) and ensure all data handling exceeds GDPR standards.
Cloud Efficiency & Governance: Drive FinOps initiatives through serverless conversions and resource rightsizing while maintaining high-quality documentation for architecture and data lineage.

About You 

Essential experience & skills

Languages: Expert-level Python, SQL, and Shell Scripting; working knowledge of Go.
GCP Ecosystem: Deep expertise in BigQuery, Vertex AI, Dataflow, Cloud Run, GKE, and Pub/Sub.
Infrastructure: Mastery of Terraform and GitHub Actions or Gitlab
Databases: Hands-on experience with Postgres DB's, and NoSQL solutions.
Tools: Expertise in Airflow (DAG development), Looker (LookML), and API management.
Certifications: Professional GCP/AWS Cloud Architect or Data Engineer certifications are highly regarded.


Benefits



Here’s a taste of what TEG offers: 

Complimentary event tickets
Birthday and volunteering leave
Wellbeing discounts & flu vaccinations
Paid parental leave & free employee support (EAP)
Global rewards and recognition
Learning, development & career pathways
A diverse, inclusive, and passionate team

Equal Opportunities

TEG is an equal opportunity employer committed to embrace diversity, respect, and care for our people and communities. 

If there are any adjustments that need to be made to ensure you have a fair and equitable experience in our recruitment process, please advise us when scheduling your interview. 

*Only direct applications will be considered. No recruiters please*

Company Description

Nine is Australia’s largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you’ll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country’s most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands. 

Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.

Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We’re evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.

Job Description

At Nine, we are harnessing the power of Artificial Intelligence to revolutionise our audience engagement and commercial strategies. As Head of AI Product, you will be at the forefront of this transformation, driving the development and implementation of innovative AI-powered products across our diverse portfolio.

This role is pivotal in shaping Nine’s AI strategy, translating complex AI capabilities into tangible product features that deliver exceptional value to our audiences, advertisers, and internal teams. You will be responsible for defining the product vision, building the roadmap, and leading a cross-functional team to deliver cutting-edge AI solutions.

Reporting to the Director, AI and Data Product, you will work closely with data scientists, engineers, and business stakeholders to identify and capitalise on opportunities to leverage AI in our media landscape.

As Head of AI Product, you will:

Define and champion the AI product vision and strategy: Align AI initiatives with Nine’s overall business objectives, identifying key opportunities to leverage AI for innovation and competitive advantage.

Lead the development of AI-powered products: Drive the end-to-end product lifecycle, from ideation and prototyping to launch and iteration, ensuring products are aligned with user needs and deliver measurable value.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams: Work closely with data scientists, engineers, designers, and business stakeholders to define requirements, prioritise features, and deliver high-quality AI solutions.

Build and maintain the AI product roadmap: Prioritise initiatives based on business impact, technical feasibility, and customer feedback, ensuring a clear and achievable roadmap.

Drive AI adoption across the organization: Evangelise the potential of AI, providing guidance and resources to teams across Nine to explore and integrate AI capabilities into their workflows.

Implement AI governance and ethical practices: Ensure AI products are developed and deployed responsibly, adhering to ethical guidelines and data privacy regulations

Analyse and measure product performance: Define and track key metrics to evaluate the success of AI products, using data-driven insights to inform product decisions and drive continuous improvement.

Stay ahead of the curve: Continuously research and evaluate emerging AI technologies and trends, identifying opportunities to incorporate them into Nine’s product strategy.

Qualifications

We are seeking a passionate and driven product leader with a strong understanding of AI and its potential to transform the media industry. You will have a proven track record of delivering successful AI products and a deep understanding of the product development lifecycle.

What you’ll bring to the role:

Experience:

5+ years of experience in product management, with a focus on AI or machine learning products.
Experience working in a media, technology, or data-driven organisation.
Experience leading cross-functional teams and managing complex projects.
Demonstrated experience bringing AI products from conception to launch.

Skills:

Deep understanding of AI and machine learning concepts and technologies.
Strong product vision and strategic thinking, with the ability to translate business objectives into actionable product roadmaps.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate with diverse teams.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making.
Understanding of data governance, privacy, and ethical considerations related to AI.Familiarity with agile product development methodologies.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Masters or PhD in AI/ML is highly desirable.
Certifications in AI product management or machine learning are advantageous.
Additional Information

How we work

At Nine, our flexible work options vary by role and team. Depending on the position, this may include flexible hours, hybrid work, or part-time arrangements. We welcome discussing your flexibility needs during the hiring process - just ask the Talent Acquisition team. 

Our employee benefits include:

18 weeks paid parental leave with no distinction between primary and secondary carers.
Access to 'Employee Exclusives' program - a way of getting closer to our incredible brands, offering unique experiences, behind-the-scenes access, and awesome perks.
Digital newspaper subscription to our mastheads.
Annual gift voucher for Stan subscription.

More info at Nine Careers.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

We're committed to a safe, respectful and inclusive Nine. From day one, you'll be encouraged to bring your whole self to work and will be supported to perform at your best.

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, and of all ages, genders, nationalities, backgrounds and cultures as we recognise the importance and value of diverse perspectives. Should you require any adjustments to the recruitment process, please advise us when you apply.

Work rights: Please note to apply for this role you must already have the right to lawfully work and live in Australia.

ABOUT TL CONSULTING




At TL Consulting, we simplify modern complexity with advanced, sustainable engineering. As a proudly Australian-owned consultancy with over 15 years of experience, we empower businesses to innovate and achieve measurable outcomes through scalable, cloud-native solutions. Our 50+ consultants operate across key sectors including banking, financial services & insurance (BFSI), government, retail, education, and entertainment. This cross-industry expertise allows us to deliver customer-centric solutions that combine deep technical capability with a clear understanding of each client’s strategic vision. Our approach leverages proven, scalable blueprints tailored to your business direction- enabling accelerated outcomes, maximized ROI, and sustainable long-term adoption.




We maintain strategic partnerships with organizations that share our vision for a cloud-native future. Through our collaborations with Microsoft, GitHub, and Databricks, we ensure our capabilities stay aligned with evolving best practices. These partnerships also provide access to innovation programs, co-funding opportunities, and dedicated support—helping us accelerate time-to-value for our clients.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The AI Engineering Practice Lead is responsible for building and leading the organisation’s AI Engineering capability, focusing on AI-driven software development, developer productivity, and enterprise AI integration.

This role combines technical leadership, practice development, and delivery oversight, ensuring the organisation can successfully design, implement, and scale AI-enabled engineering solutions such as GitHub Copilot adoption, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, AI developer tooling, and AI-assisted software delivery platforms.

The Practice Lead defines service offerings, develops technical standards, mentors engineers, and supports strategic client engagements.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Practice Leadership

Build and lead the AI Engineering practice within the Cloud & DevSecOps business unit.

Define the practice vision, roadmap, and service offerings aligned with market demand.

Establish technical standards, frameworks, and reference architectures for AI-enabled engineering.

Develop reusable accelerators, patterns, and assets to support delivery at scale.

Grow the capability through recruitment, training, and mentoring of AI engineers.




AI Engineering & Developer Productivity: Lead initiatives focused on AI-assisted software engineering, including:

Enterprise adoption of AI coding assistants

AI-driven developer productivity platforms

AI-enhanced CI/CD workflows

AI agents supporting engineering tasks

Integration of LLMs into developer workflows

Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations

AI-enabled developer platforms and internal tools




Client Engagement & Delivery:

Act as the technical lead for AI engineering engagements.

Support solution architecture and design for AI-enabled engineering platforms.

Provide technical guidance to delivery teams across projects.

Ensure best practices in AI governance, security, and responsible AI adoption.

Contribute to complex architecture decisions involving AI platforms and developer tooling.




Pre-Sales & Thought Leadership

Support business development and pre-sales activities for AI engineering opportunities.

Collaborate with sales and consulting teams to shape AI-focused solutions and proposals.

Represent the organisation as a subject matter expert in AI-assisted engineering.

Contribute to industry thought leadership, internal knowledge sharing, and practice development.







SKILLS & EXPERIENCE




Required Skills and Qualifications:

Education: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field. A Master’s degree or relevant certifications are a plus.




Experience:

10+ years of experience in consulting, system integration, or IT project management.

Strong experience in several of the following areas:

AI-assisted software development tools

Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI integration

AI developer tooling and automation

Git-based development platforms

CI/CD platforms and DevOps practices

Cloud-native architectures

Company: Kala Data

About Kala

Kala Data is building energy-backed compute infrastructure by colocating modular data centres with low-cost power generation. We currently operate multiple sites running high-density compute workloads and are transitioning these into GPU-based infrastructure for AI and high-performance computing.

Our core thesis is that energy is local, compute is global — and by controlling power, we can deliver materially lower-cost compute.

Kala is a funded business with active deployments and expansion underway, providing a strong foundation to scale both infrastructure and platform capabilities.

The Opportunity

We are looking for an engineer to build the next stage of our platform — a system that allows customers to access GPU compute through Kala, while dynamically provisioning capacity across both Kala-owned infrastructure and third-party providers.

This is not a typical SaaS role. You will be building a thin orchestration layer over distributed GPU supply, solving real-world infrastructure problems across energy, compute, and networking.

What You’ll Be Building

You will lead the development of a platform that:

Accepts customer requests for GPU compute (via API and/or simple UI)
Routes workloads to available Kala-owned GPU infrastructure
Dynamically provisions external GPU capacity when local supply is constrained (e.g. via third-party APIs)
Abstracts away infrastructure differences so users experience a single, unified platform
Tracks usage, performance, and cost across multiple compute providers

Over time, this system will evolve into a fully integrated GPU platform combining owned and third-party capacity.

Key Responsibilities

Design and build the core job routing and scheduling layer
Integrate with external GPU providers via API (provisioning, monitoring, teardown)
Build a clean abstraction layer for compute resources
Develop APIs for users to request and manage workloads
Implement monitoring, logging, and cost tracking across infrastructure
Work closely with the founders on product direction and system design

What We’re Looking For

Strong backend engineering experience (Python, Go, or similar)
Experience building APIs and distributed systems
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or similar)
Experience with containers (Docker) and ideally orchestration (Kubernetes)
Comfortable working with infrastructure-level problems (networking, compute, resource allocation)
Ability to operate independently and make architectural decisions

Nice to have:

Experience with GPU workloads or ML infrastructure
Experience with job schedulers (e.g. Slurm, Kubernetes jobs)
Familiarity with marketplace or multi-provider systems

Why This Role

You will be building the core system that underpins the business
Direct exposure to energy + compute infrastructure (not just cloud abstraction)
Opportunity to shape architecture from day one
High ownership and autonomy

Compensation

Competitive salary + equity
Flexible depending on experience
